name: Apple HIG Designer MCP
what_it_does: Apple HIG Designer MCP server enabling AI agents to query Apple's Human Interface Guidelines — retrieving design principles, UI component specifications, platform-specific guidelines, and accessibility recommendations, integrating Apple's official design documentation into agent-driven iOS/macOS UI design and development workflows.
use_cases: Querying Apple HIG for UI component design specifications from design agents
Retrieving platform-specific design guidelines from iOS/macOS development agents
Checking accessibility requirements from compliance review agents
Getting SF Symbol usage guidelines from icon design agents
Validating UI patterns against Apple guidelines from code review agents
Integrating HIG knowledge into AI-assisted SwiftUI development
not_for: Non-Apple platform design (Android, web — use Material Design or W3C docs instead)
Live design generation (provides guidelines, not visual assets)
Teams not developing for Apple platforms
best_when: An iOS/macOS developer needs quick access to Apple HIG specifications during AI-assisted SwiftUI or UIKit development — real-time design compliance checking.
avoid_when: You're developing for Android, web, or cross-platform — use platform-appropriate design documentation MCPs.

auth.notes: No authentication — local MCP server with bundled or scraped Apple HIG content. No external API calls required.

pricing.notes: Free open source MCP. Apple HIG is publicly available documentation.

known_agent_gotchas: HIG content may not be kept current with latest Apple platform releases
Community MCP — content accuracy depends on how frequently it's updated
Niche tool focused on Apple HIG — limited query types
Apple HIG is extensive — search quality depends on indexing approach
Not an official Apple MCP — content sourced from public documentation
agent_readiness.security_notes: Local MCP. No credentials. Community tool. Apple HIG is public documentation — no privacy concerns.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local only. No credentials. Public documentation. Community MCP.
